title: rosa rubiginosa subsp. sepium
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subsp. sepium, also known as the scotch rose or wild briar, is a subspecies of the fragrant rose plant native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant compounds, which have been found to have numerous health benefits. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subsp. sepium include reducing inflammation, improving skin and wound healing, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in skincare products, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
